data.title: Document commit message convention: prefix with Lattice short ID

data.body:
Reviewed commit 0c21147 against plan .lattice/plans/task_01KQW6VW44Q2CZFM6EM328RFXZ.md.

Acceptance criteria:
- New '### Commit Messages' subsection present in both CLAUDE.md (line 159) and agents.md (line 157). Confirmed.
- Section bodies are byte-identical between the two files (verified via git show diff and direct read). Confirmed.
- Placement is exactly between '### Branch Linking' and '### Leave Breadcrumbs' in both files. Confirmed.
- Content states the rule, gives an example ('TYB-11: docs(lattice): add commit message convention'), and explains *why* in one line (stitches git history to the Lattice event log so a future reader can map any commit back to task/plan/review). Also adds a sensible nuance for multi-task commits.
- The implementing commit subject 'TYB-11: docs(lattice): require commit messages to be prefixed with task short ID' follows the new convention.

TYB-10 metadata bundling: The commit also includes TYB-10's per-task files plus shared _lifecycle.jsonl / ids.json updates. Verified that the _lifecycle.jsonl and ids.json entries for TYB-10 came from a dashboard:web actor predating this session, so bundling the per-task files alongside is consistent-state housekeeping rather than scope creep. Commit body acknowledges this clearly.

No issues found.

Verdict: Pass — no issues found.
